name: Knuth-Morris-Pratt
def: Linear-time substring search that never re-examines text characters, using a prefix-failure table.
problem: Find a pattern in text in O(n+m) without the quadratic backtracking of naive search.
mechanism: Precompute a failure function (longest proper prefix that is also a suffix) so mismatches shift the pattern without rescanning matched text.
limits: Boyer-Moore often faster in practice for large alphabets
failures: Assuming sublinear average (it is linear, not sublinear)
whenNot: Large-alphabet text where Boyer-Moore skips faster
myths: "KMP is the fastest matcher" — Boyer-Moore/its variants often win in practice
